request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will the SEC officially classify Ether as a security by 2026-12-31?
Kind: active
Timeline (20 events):
- 2026-05-19 [background] CFTC Staff Issues Advisory on Cooperation in Enforcement Matters — CFTC advisory on enforcement cooperation is tangential—may marginally enable cross‑agency referrals but doesn't materially affect SEC classifying Ether.
- 2026-05-19 [background] Registered Offering Reform — Generic SEC rulemaking on registered offerings doesn't explicitly classify ETH; it only marginally affects ETH-as-security odds via broader token-treatment changes.
- 2026-05-18 [C] Rescission of Policy Regarding Denials in Settlements of Enforcement Actions — Rescinding ‘denials-in-settlements’ policy makes it easier for the SEC to obtain enforceable findings/orders in settlements, raising chance ETH is declared a security.
- 2026-05-13 [background] CME | Expansion of the Trading and ClearingHours for all Cryptocurrency Futures and Options on Futures Contracts. | [1] — CFTC expanding crypto futures/clearing hours reinforces commodity-market treatment of crypto, slightly reducing likelihood SEC will classify ETH as a security.
- 2026-04-13 [background] Staff Statement Regarding Broker-Dealer Registration of Certain User Interfaces Utilized to Prepare Transactions in Crypto Asset Securities — SEC staff guidance treating certain crypto interfaces as tied to 'crypto asset securities' modestly raises chance ETH will be treated as a security, but it's not binding.
- 2026-04-08 [background] SEC Appoints David Woodcock as Director of the Division of Enforcement — New SEC enforcement director could slightly shift enforcement posture toward crypto, but record gives no Ether-specific or policy clues.
- 2026-04-08 [background] U.S.-UK Financial Regulatory Working Group Winter 2026: Joint Statement — Joint U.S.-UK regulatory statement includes the SEC and signals coordinated crypto oversight — a modest background signal but not decisive about ETH classification.
- 2026-03-23 [B] 91 FR 13714 - 3/23/2026 - Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets — SEC Federal Register publication on applying securities laws to crypto directly raises the chance ETH will be declared a security.
- 2026-03-23 [B] 91 FR 13714 - 3/23/2026 - Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets (PDF) — SEC published a Federal Register entry on applying securities laws to crypto; if it’s a final rule covering ETH, it would directly determine YES.
- 2026-03-18 [background] Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets and Certain Transactions Involving Crypto Assets Corporation Finance — An SEC staff statement on applying securities laws to crypto assets makes it more likely ETH will be treated as a security by clarifying coverage and enabling enforcement.
- 2026-03-17 [B] Regulation Crypto Assets: A Token Safe Harbor — SEC's 2026 'Token Safe Harbor' signals rulemaking to exempt compliant tokens from securities law, reducing the chance ETH is formally declared a security.
- 2026-02-17 [background] 91 FR 7264 — A CFTC Federal Register action may signal a commodity classification for ETH, modestly lowering odds the SEC will label ETH a security.
- 2026-02-12 [background] CFE Cboe Ether Continuous Futures Lead Market Maker Program Amendment [2] — CFTC approval/amendment of Cboe Ether futures program reinforces commodity framing of ETH, slightly lowering SEC-security odds.
- 2026-02-03 [background] ICE US - New Cryptocurrency Futures Market Maker Program — CFTC program for crypto futures reinforces commodity framing of ETH, slightly lowering odds the SEC will formally classify ETH as a security.
- 2026-02-03 [background] ICE US - New Cryptocurrency Futures Liquidity Provider Program — A CFTC/ICE futures liquidity program reinforces commodity-framing of crypto, a slight bearish signal for SEC classifying ETH as a security.
- 2026-01-22 [background] ICE US | New Cryptocurrency Futures Maker Program — CFTC-backed ICE futures maker program reinforces commodity-market treatment of crypto, slightly lowering odds SEC will deem ETH a security.
- 2026-01-21 [background] CME — CFTC/CME action invoking the Commodity Exchange Act could imply commodity treatment for ETH, making an SEC security finding less likely, but details are absent.
- 2026-01-21 [background] 91 FR 2523 — A CFTC Federal Register action on Ether likely signals CFTC commodity stance, which makes an SEC security determination less likely (low-impact).
- 2025-12-17 [background] 90 FR 58525 — A CFTC Federal Register action signals inter-agency framing of crypto as commodities, an indirect weak signal lowering odds SEC will call ETH a security.
- 2025-08-04 [background] Acting Chairman Pham Launches Listed Spot Crypto Trading Initiative — CFTC initiative favors CFTC oversight of spot crypto, modestly lowering odds the SEC will formally deem Ether a security.

response.text: Since 2025, CFTC moves (FR notices, CME/Cboe/ICE programs, longer crypto trading hours) treat ETH as a commodity, damping SEC‑security odds. SEC pushes coverage via staff statements, a 3/23/26 Federal Register item on applying securities laws, and a tougher settlements policy; yet a Token Safe Harbor cuts the other way. No official SEC classification yet; outcome hinges on any finalized rule or decisive enforcement before 2026‑12‑31.
